checks.a_analysis: The output content closely follows the source document. The subsection titles like 'Roll Call (David Newberry)' or 'NMAC (Greg Dotson)' are derived directly from headings found within the document pages. No fabricated body text or invented sections were identified.

All dates (June 9, 2022; March 10, 2022; July 14, 2022) and links (arcgis.com dashboard) match the source document exactly. No phone numbers or email addresses were present in the source or fabricated in the output.
The conversion includes all 8 pages of the source PDF. The agenda (P1), minutes (P2-P4), and detailed agenda notes/slides (P5-P8) are all represented in the JSON structure.

All tables in the document were verified for row count. The Agenda table (10 rows), Attendance table (32 rows), Guests table (9 rows), Minutes Vote table (12 rows), Migrations table (3 items), and Deployment Service Types table (5 rows) all match the source document's data rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: No significant text omissions were found. Detailed lists (e.g., the 4 jurisdictions in the GIS update on P7) and project notes (Military Sites, Cybersecurity on P6) are fully captured.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Minor OCR artifacts like 'Macon Count' (missing 'y' from page 6) and 'Jeff Ship' (missing 'p' from page 1) are accurately reflected in the output as they appear in the source text/OCR. The informational content is preserved perfectly.

checks.a_analysis: The output successfully avoids unintended content duplication. While certain strings like 'Next Meeting: July 14, 2022' appear twice, this faithfully reflects the source document where the text is present on both page 1 and page 8. Similarly, the repetitive document headers ('North Carolina 911 Board...') present on every page of the PDF have been appropriately deduplicated or treated as section headings, which is an acceptable structural change.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. The document title is an H2, major subsections like 'Attendance' are H3s, and subsequent topical sections from the presentation (e.g., 'Roll Call', 'Ethics Awareness', 'GIS Update') are H2s. This structure enables clear navigation and correctly identifies the major divisions of the source material without skipping levels.

The table structure contains major corruptions in the Attendance section. In the first Attendance table (Page 2), the header cell 'Phone/Teams' is assigned a 'colspan' of 2. This incorrectly merges the 'Name' column (Members/Staff) with the first status column under the 'Phone/Teams' header, making the name column semantically incorrect for screen readers. Furthermore, the second part of the Attendance table (Page 3) identifies its first data row ('Brian Eaton -- AT&T') as a header row ('is_header_row': true), which is a significant semantic error as it contains data (a guest name).
checks.b_evidence_source: Attendance table on Page 2 and Page 3. Page 3 first row: 'Brian Eaton -- AT&T'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element index 5 (Table 2): first header cell has 'colspan': 2. Element index 6 (Table 3): first row is marked 'is_header_row': true.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the converted document matches the source PDF exactly. It flows from the Agenda (Page 1) to the Minutes (Pages 2-4) and then into the detailed presentation slides (Pages 5-8). The logical progression of information is preserved.

Lists are generally converted correctly. The ordered lists for meeting minutes and the nested unordered lists for project updates (Military, Cybersecurity, GIS) accurately reflect the hierarchical structure and intent of the source document.

The output fails to identify and inject functional links for several clear hyperlinks present in the source document. Specifically, underlined text such as 'NC NG911 Deployment Map' (Page 6), 'NC Next Generation 911 GIS Services Webpage' (Page 7), and 'NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com)' (Page 7) are converted as standard text paragraphs rather than interactive link elements.
checks.b_evidence_source: NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com) on Page 7.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text is present in a paragraph element (index 19) but is not a link object.

checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML document follows the visual flow and organization of the source PDF. The agenda, attendance tables, minutes, and status updates are presented in the same order. Columnar layouts for the agenda and tables are correctly preserved in the HTML version, ensuring that the visual relationship between topics/names and their corresponding presenters/attendance status is maintained.

All tables from the source PDF, including the Agenda, Attendance, Minutes Vote, Next Up Migrations, and Current Deployment Service Types tables, are rendered correctly in the HTML. They maintain distinct rows and columns, clear headers, and readable content alignment. The empty cells in the 'Next Up Migrations' table from page 5 are faithfully reproduced in the HTML layout.

The NC DIT logo appearing on multiple pages of the source PDF is present in the HTML output. Additionally, the bar chart titled 'Summary of PSAPS Live on ESInet' from page 6 of the PDF is correctly included and rendered in the HTML version.

There is no evidence of body content being duplicated in the HTML output. Section headings appear in the Table of Contents and correctly transition into their respective body sections, which is standard accessible design and not a defect. No paragraphs or tables are repeated unnecessarily.
